title: transport: relay_socket_addr doc claims SocketAddr return type
description:
In core/src/transport.rs, the doc comment for relay_socket_addr (line 463) reads:

  /// Resolve the relay host:port to a `SocketAddr` for a direct TCP connection.

But the function signature is:
  fn relay_socket_addr(relay_url: &str) -> Result<String>

It returns a String (formatted as "host:port"), not a std::net::SocketAddr. The doc is misleading — a reader might expect a resolved IP-level SocketAddr, but the function returns a hostname string that may still require DNS resolution by TcpStream::connect.

Fix: update the doc to say 'Returns a "host:port" string' and note that DNS resolution happens at connect time.
